comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Code-is-cheap is a brand new paradigm that humans need to get their head around as they start working with the current generation of LLM agents.  You can't apply it naively, though.  The hard work doesn't change.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Very good observation. The unstated assumption was the most core thing that I brought to the table! I didn't bring it up during our port because I swam in that water for three weeks during my initial implementation.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: I'm glad you recognize that! I don't think I made a mistake, per se, in choosing to use the wire format. The mistake was more subtle--I forgot that I was making a very important tradeoff that should have been explicit from the beginning.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Correct. This is why we always have to be turning up the analysis knob. We can rip out code and write in on a time-frame of an hour once we have clarity. It's the 4:1 ratio here that's central.
